slides.title: A daily handful of nuts, *a longer life*.
slides.sub: One of the simplest food habits is linked to aging well. The signal is surprisingly strong.

slides.title: Small habit, big signal
slides.body: People who eat nuts regularly *tend to live* longer in large studies. A tiny habit carries real weight.

slides.title: What nuts give you
slides.body: Healthy fats, fiber and minerals come packed in one bite. Together they *can support* the heart and steady energy.

slides.title: Just a small handful
slides.body: About a closed handful a day is plenty. Nuts are rich, so a *little goes far*.

slides.title: Simple ways to add them
slides.body: Stir them into oats, salads or yogurt, or keep a small jar nearby for a *grab and go* snack.

slides.title: An easy daily win
slides.body: Keep nuts within reach and enjoy a handful most days. It is a small habit that *may add up*.

slides.title: Build habits that *add good years*.
slides.body: Vibe Health tracks the food, sleep and movement that help you age well, all in one simple daily score.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
A daily handful of nuts is one of the simplest longevity habits there is. 🌿

The healthy fats, fiber and minerals support your heart and energy, and the signal in the research is strong. A small handful is all it takes. Save this. 🔖

Vibe Health turns those food habits into one daily score → link in bio.
youtube_title: Why a Daily Handful of Nuts Is Linked to a Longer Life
